customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety — William is pre-sold, technically engaged, and confirmed without friction. Primary risk is format confusion (in-person vs. phone) and unrepresentative bill data, not customer resistance.
unresolved_concerns: Bill data is atypically low ($90.80 / 80 kWh) because William has not been occupying the home — 'I haven't been there.' The graph photo was captured but the usage does not reflect normal occupancy. Closer should ask William about typical usage when the home is fully occupied before sizing the system.

Appointment format ambiguity — rep used in-person language ('pop by,' 'out in your neighborhood'). William confirmed with 'I'll be here,' which may mean he expects a physical visit. Closer should confirm format at the start of the call.
sensitive_topics_raised: Hearing impairment — William disclosed he was not wearing his hearing aid during the call. Closer should speak clearly and check for comprehension, particularly during the proposal walkthrough.
exact_promises_rep_made: Rep stated a specialist would 'pop by' and 'go over the numbers' — in-person framing used.
closer_should_know: William is technically knowledgeable (DIY camper solar, knows panel wattage and inverter specs) and pre-sold on whole-house solar — he will engage on system sizing details. His bill is unrepresentative because he hasn't been living in the home; ask about normal occupancy usage before presenting the design. He has a hearing impairment — speak clearly and confirm he's following the proposal.
